howItWorks.intro: This european energy independence timeline estimator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Annual dependency reduction = renewable growth × 0.3 + LNG expansion × 0.2 + demand reduction
Years to independence = current dependency / annual reduction
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How dependent is Europe on Russian energy?
faq.answer: Pre-2022, the EU imported ~40% of gas and ~25% of oil from Russia. By 2024, Russian gas dropped to ~15% of EU supply through LNG imports, demand reduction, and renewable expansion. Full independence remains years away.

faq.question: Is the European Energy Independence Timeline Estimator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard geopolitics and energy markets formulas trusted by analysts and informed citizens. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What renewable growth rate should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate renewable growth rate value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the European Energy Independence Timeline Estimator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the European Energy Independence Timeline Estimator?
sections.body: The European Energy Independence Timeline Estimator is a free online tool designed for analysts and informed citizens who need quick, accurate calculations in the geopolitics and energy markets space. By entering your renewable growth rate, lng import expansion, demand reduction, you get instant results including current dependency, gas independence year, oil independence year. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting current dependency right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In geopolitics and energy markets, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by analysts and informed citizens worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand global dynamics with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your renewable growth rate and need to find the right current dependency. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Renewable Growth Rate (%/yr)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your renewable growth rate in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your LNG Import Expansion (%/yr)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your lng import expansion in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Demand Reduction (%/yr)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your demand reduction in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your current dependency, gas independence year, oil independence year. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your renewable growth rate before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both current dependency and gas independence year — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to understand global dynamics.
If you're unsure about your demand reduction, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
